visa_types.name: Family Or Friends Visit
visa_types.documents_required:
Click here to download the checklist. (#)
Letter of Guarantee from a Legal Person in Croatia. Click Here (https://mvep.gov.hr/UserDocsImages/dokumenti/obrasci/211104-Jamstveno-pismo-pra.pdf?vel=314551)
This must be stamped by legal person and certified by notary public in Croatia. Original Letter of Guarantee must be submitted for Visa Application.
visa_types.visa_fees:
Fees are due on the visa application when applying.

[Table:   Family Or Friends Visit]
Service Fee:
- Apart from the visa fee, a
 VFS service charge of NIS 139.47 
inclusive of VAT per application will be levied for applicants applying at the visa application centre.
- The applicable visa fee in NIS/ILS is as per the current exchange rate. It is subject to change without notice due to sudden foreign exchange fluctuations.
- All fees are non-refundable.
The following are exempt from airport-transit (visa A) and short-term visa (visa C) fees upon application:
- Family members of a Croatian national (spouse and children),
- Family members of citizens of EEA Member States and Switzerland,
- Children up to the age of 12,
- Holders of diplomatic and service passports travelling in their official capacity
- Pupils, students, postgraduate students and teachers accompanying them when they come to attend school, study and professional training,
- Researchers coming to conduct research, as provided for by the Recommendation of the European Parliament and of the Council of 28 September 2005 to facilitate the issue by the Member States of uniform short-stay visas for researchers from third countries travelling within the Community for the purpose of carrying out scientific research,
- Representatives of non-profit organizations up to the age of 25, who are to attend seminars, conferences, sport, cultural or educational events organized by non-profit organizations.
visa_types.processing_time:
The average processing time for the visa application is 30 calendar days at the Embassy. The Visa Application along with Travel Document is sent to the Embassy of Croatia in Tel Aviv for consideration and processing. However, in case the documents are not submitted as per requirement or due to other factors or justified reasons, the processing time may be extended up to 60 days.
Application logistics time apart from Embassy's own processing time may vary and may affect the total processing time of the visa applications. 
Eid, Summer holidays (July and August), National Holidays, Christmas, and New Year may extend the visa application processing time. VFS/Mission’s closures and holidays may extend the visa application processing time.
Please be advised that due to the above-mentioned minimum processing time, we are not able to guarantee the return of passports by specific dates for travel.
IMPORTANT NOTE:
To avoid any delays in the visa application process, it is paramount that the visa application is complete, and the documents submitted are as per the Mission’s requirements. The Embassy of Croatia may request additional documents, and this may lead to further delay in visa application processing time.
Applications will be accepted maximum 90 days prior to the travel date.
Please be sure to submit your visa application well in advance, to avoid any inconveniences.
visa_types.forms:
Visa Application Form duly filled Online only in English & signed by the applicant.
(For minors under age of 18: signed by parent(s) or legal guardian)
Online Application Form (https://crovisa.mvep.hr/?lang=en)
- Uploading Photograph is a must.
- Uploading the supporting documents as per the document checklist is a must.
- All items on the application are mandatory;
- Hostel/ Hotel Details and Contact Details
- Contact Details for the applicant.
- Not Uploading documents will affect your visa application decision.
visa_types.overview:
Overview
Short-term visa (visa C) - approval for transit through or intended stay in the territory of Croatia for a duration of no more than 90 days in any 180-day period.
It is issued for one, two or more entries for transit, tourist, business, private or other purposes. The validity period of a visa depends on the circumstances of traveling and cannot exceed five years.
The third-country national may not work in the territory of Croatia based on a short-term visa (visa C) alone.
Short-term visa (visa C) application shall be submitted in person. Visa application shall be submitted no more than six months.
All third-country nationals who are holders of valid Multiple Entry Schengen visa, as well as national visas and residence permits of Bulgaria, Cyprus, and Romania do not require an additional (Croatian) visa for Croatia. They can transit or stay in the territory of Croatia not exceeding 90 days in any 180-day period.
For Business and Private Visits, on every crossing of the border, the police may require the foreign citizen to present all relevant documents that includes copies of the Letters of Guarantee.
visa_types.full_text:
## Overview
Short-term visa (visa C) - approval for transit through or intended stay in the territory of Croatia for a duration of no more than 90 days in any 180-day period.
It is issued for one, two or more entries for transit, tourist, business, private or other purposes. The validity period of a visa depends on the circumstances of traveling and cannot exceed five years.
The third-country national may not work in the territory of Croatia based on a short-term visa (visa C) alone.
Short-term visa (visa C) application shall be submitted in person. Visa application shall be submitted no more than six months.
All third-country nationals who are holders of valid Multiple Entry Schengen visa, as well as national visas and residence permits of Bulgaria, Cyprus, and Romania do not require an additional (Croatian) visa for Croatia. They can transit or stay in the territory of Croatia not exceeding 90 days in any 180-day period.
For Business and Private Visits, on every crossing of the border, the police may require the foreign citizen to present all relevant documents that includes copies of the Letters of Guarantee.

## Visa Fees
Fees are due on the visa application when applying.

[Table:   Family Or Friends Visit]
Service Fee:
- Apart from the visa fee, a
 VFS service charge of NIS 139.47 
inclusive of VAT per application will be levied for applicants applying at the visa application centre.
- The applicable visa fee in NIS/ILS is as per the current exchange rate. It is subject to change without notice due to sudden foreign exchange fluctuations.
- All fees are non-refundable.
The following are exempt from airport-transit (visa A) and short-term visa (visa C) fees upon application:
- Family members of a Croatian national (spouse and children),
- Family members of citizens of EEA Member States and Switzerland,
- Children up to the age of 12,
- Holders of diplomatic and service passports travelling in their official capacity
- Pupils, students, postgraduate students and teachers accompanying them when they come to attend school, study and professional training,
- Researchers coming to conduct research, as provided for by the Recommendation of the European Parliament and of the Council of 28 September 2005 to facilitate the issue by the Member States of uniform short-stay visas for researchers from third countries travelling within the Community for the purpose of carrying out scientific research,
- Representatives of non-profit organizations up to the age of 25, who are to attend seminars, conferences, sport, cultural or educational events organized by non-profit organizations.

## Documents Required
Click here to download the checklist. (#)
Letter of Guarantee from a Legal Person in Croatia. Click Here (https://mvep.gov.hr/UserDocsImages/dokumenti/obrasci/211104-Jamstveno-pismo-pra.pdf?vel=314551)
This must be stamped by legal person and certified by notary public in Croatia. Original Letter of Guarantee must be submitted for Visa Application.

## Photo specifications
Photographs must follow the below guidelines
- in colour
- 35x45 mm
- printed on normal photographic paper
- taken against a light background
- full face taken straight forward 30-36 mm from point of the chin to the head of hair
- the person is to be captured with neutral facial expression, with his/her eyes open and mouth closed
- The photograph of a person, who for religious, ethnic or medical reasons is wearing a head cover, must show the person's cheeks, chin and forehead
- children in photographs must be alone, and their faces fully visible and eyes open
Note: Please follow these instructions carefully. If the photos provided do not follow these requirements your application will be considered incomplete!

## Processing Time
The average processing time for the visa application is 30 calendar days at the Embassy. The Visa Application along with Travel Document is sent to the Embassy of Croatia in Tel Aviv for consideration and processing. However, in case the documents are not submitted as per requirement or due to other factors or justified reasons, the processing time may be extended up to 60 days.
Application logistics time apart from Embassy's own processing time may vary and may affect the total processing time of the visa applications. 
Eid, Summer holidays (July and August), National Holidays, Christmas, and New Year may extend the visa application processing time. VFS/Mission’s closures and holidays may extend the visa application processing time.
Please be advised that due to the above-mentioned minimum processing time, we are not able to guarantee the return of passports by specific dates for travel.
IMPORTANT NOTE:
To avoid any delays in the visa application process, it is paramount that the visa application is complete, and the documents submitted are as per the Mission’s requirements. The Embassy of Croatia may request additional documents, and this may lead to further delay in visa application processing time.
Applications will be accepted maximum 90 days prior to the travel date.
Please be sure to submit your visa application well in advance, to avoid any inconveniences.

## Download Forms
Visa Application Form duly filled Online only in English & signed by the applicant.
(For minors under age of 18: signed by parent(s) or legal guardian)
Online Application Form (https://crovisa.mvep.hr/?lang=en)
- Uploading Photograph is a must.
- Uploading the supporting documents as per the document checklist is a must.
- All items on the application are mandatory;
- Hostel/ Hotel Details and Contact Details
- Contact Details for the applicant.
- Not Uploading documents will affect your visa application decision.

visa_types.name: LONG TERM (VISA D) - Work
visa_types.documents_required: Click here to download the checklist. (#)
visa_types.visa_fees:
Fees are due on the visa application when applying.

[Table:   LONG TERM (VISA D) -  Work]
Service Fee:
- Apart from the visa fee, a
 VFS service charge of NIS 139.47 
inclusive of VAT per application will be levied for applicants applying at the visa application centre.
- The applicable visa fee in NIS/ILS is as per the current exchange rate. It is subject to change without notice due to sudden foreign exchange fluctuations.
- All fees are non-refundable.
The following are exempt from long-term visa (visa D) fees upon application:
- children up to the age of 12,
- family reunification – exempted in the case of family members of a Croatian national (spouse, children)
- third-country nationals who have been granted the temporary stay for the following purposes:
- secondary school education
- university studies
- research
- humanitarian grounds.
visa_types.processing_time:
The average processing time for the visa application is 30 calendar days at the Embassy. The Visa Application along with Travel Document is sent to the Embassy of Croatia in Tel Aviv for consideration and processing. However, in case the documents are not submitted as per requirement or due to other factors or justified reasons, the processing time may be extended up to 60 days.
Application logistics time apart from Embassy's own processing time may vary and may affect the total processing time of the visa applications. 
Eid, Summer holidays (July and August), National Holidays, Christmas, and New Year may extend the visa application processing time. VFS/Mission’s closures and holidays may extend the visa application processing time.
Please be advised that due to the above-mentioned minimum processing time, we are not able to guarantee the return of passports by specific dates for travel.
IMPORTANT NOTE:
To avoid any delays in the visa application process, it is paramount that the visa application is complete, and the documents submitted are as per the Mission’s requirements. The Embassy of Croatia may request additional documents, and this may lead to further delay in visa application processing time.
Applications will be accepted maximum 90 days prior to the travel date.
Please be sure to submit your visa application well in advance, to avoid any inconveniences.
visa_types.forms:
Visa Application Form duly filled Online only in English & signed by the applicant.
(For minors under age of 18: signed by parent(s) or legal guardian)
Online Application Form (https://crovisa.mvep.hr/?lang=en)
- Uploading Photograph is a must.
- Uploading the supporting documents as per the document checklist is a must.
- All items on the application are mandatory;
- Hostel/ Hotel Details and Contact Details
- Contact Details for the applicant.
- Not Uploading documents will affect your visa application decision.
visa_types.overview:
Overview
A long-stay visa (D visa) is an entry permit if the third-country national has been approved for a temporary residence permit or a residence and work permit and if a visa is required to enter the Republic of Croatia.
An application for a category “D” visa is submitted in person.
visa_types.full_text:
## Overview
A long-stay visa (D visa) is an entry permit if the third-country national has been approved for a temporary residence permit or a residence and work permit and if a visa is required to enter the Republic of Croatia.
An application for a category “D” visa is submitted in person.

## Visa Fees
Fees are due on the visa application when applying.

[Table:   LONG TERM (VISA D) -  Work]
Service Fee:
- Apart from the visa fee, a
 VFS service charge of NIS 139.47 
inclusive of VAT per application will be levied for applicants applying at the visa application centre.
- The applicable visa fee in NIS/ILS is as per the current exchange rate. It is subject to change without notice due to sudden foreign exchange fluctuations.
- All fees are non-refundable.
The following are exempt from long-term visa (visa D) fees upon application:
- children up to the age of 12,
- family reunification – exempted in the case of family members of a Croatian national (spouse, children)
- third-country nationals who have been granted the temporary stay for the following purposes:
- secondary school education
- university studies
- research
- humanitarian grounds.

## Documents Required
Click here to download the checklist. (#)

## Photo specifications
Photographs must follow the below guidelines
- in colour
- 35x45 mm
- printed on normal photographic paper
- taken against a light background
- full face taken straight forward 30-36 mm from point of the chin to the head of hair
- the person is to be captured with neutral facial expression, with his/her eyes open and mouth closed
- The photograph of a person, who for religious, ethnic or medical reasons is wearing a head cover, must show the person's cheeks, chin and forehead
- children in photographs must be alone, and their faces fully visible and eyes open
Note: Please follow these instructions carefully. If the photos provided do not follow these requirements your application will be considered incomplete!

## Processing Time
The average processing time for the visa application is 30 calendar days at the Embassy. The Visa Application along with Travel Document is sent to the Embassy of Croatia in Tel Aviv for consideration and processing. However, in case the documents are not submitted as per requirement or due to other factors or justified reasons, the processing time may be extended up to 60 days.
Application logistics time apart from Embassy's own processing time may vary and may affect the total processing time of the visa applications. 
Eid, Summer holidays (July and August), National Holidays, Christmas, and New Year may extend the visa application processing time. VFS/Mission’s closures and holidays may extend the visa application processing time.
Please be advised that due to the above-mentioned minimum processing time, we are not able to guarantee the return of passports by specific dates for travel.
IMPORTANT NOTE:
To avoid any delays in the visa application process, it is paramount that the visa application is complete, and the documents submitted are as per the Mission’s requirements. The Embassy of Croatia may request additional documents, and this may lead to further delay in visa application processing time.
Applications will be accepted maximum 90 days prior to the travel date.
Please be sure to submit your visa application well in advance, to avoid any inconveniences.

## Download Forms
Visa Application Form duly filled Online only in English & signed by the applicant.
(For minors under age of 18: signed by parent(s) or legal guardian)
Online Application Form (https://crovisa.mvep.hr/?lang=en)
- Uploading Photograph is a must.
- Uploading the supporting documents as per the document checklist is a must.
- All items on the application are mandatory;
- Hostel/ Hotel Details and Contact Details
- Contact Details for the applicant.
- Not Uploading documents will affect your visa application decision.

- Pupils, students, postgraduate students and teachers accompanying them when they come to attend school, study and professional training,
- Researchers coming to conduct research, as provided for by the Recommendation of the European Parliament and of the Council of 28 September 2005 to facilitate the issue by the Member States of uniform short-stay visas for researchers from third countries travelling within the Community for the purpose of carrying out scientific research,
- Representatives of non-profit organizations up to the age of 25, who are to attend seminars, conferences, sport, cultural or educational events organized by non-profit organizations.

## Documents Required
Click here to download the checklist. (#)
Letter of Guarantee from a Legal Person in Croatia. Click Here (https://mvep.gov.hr/UserDocsImages/dokumenti/obrasci/211104-Jamstveno-pismo-pra.pdf?vel=314551)
This must be stamped by legal person and certified by notary public in Croatia. Original Letter of Guarantee must be submitted for Visa Application.

## Photo specifications
Photographs must follow the below guidelines
- in colour
- 35x45 mm
- printed on normal photographic paper
- taken against a light background
- full face taken straight forward 30-36 mm from point of the chin to the head of hair
- the person is to be captured with neutral facial expression, with his/her eyes open and mouth closed
- The photograph of a person, who for religious, ethnic or medical reasons is wearing a head cover, must show the person's cheeks, chin and forehead
- children in photographs must be alone, and their faces fully visible and eyes open
Note: Please follow these instructions carefully. If the photos provided do not follow these requirements your application will be considered incomplete!

## Processing Time
The average processing time for the visa application is 30 calendar days at the Embassy. The Visa Application along with Travel Document is sent to the Embassy of Croatia in Tel Aviv for consideration and processing. However, in case the documents are not submitted as per requirement or due to other factors or justified reasons, the processing time may be extended up to 60 days.
Application logistics time apart from Embassy's own processing time may vary and may affect the total processing time of the visa applications. 
Eid, Summer holidays (July and August), National Holidays, Christmas, and New Year may extend the visa application processing time. VFS/Mission’s closures and holidays may extend the visa application processing time.
Please be advised that due to the above-mentioned minimum processing time, we are not able to guarantee the return of passports by specific dates for travel.
IMPORTANT NOTE:
To avoid any delays in the visa application process, it is paramount that the visa application is complete, and the documents submitted are as per the Mission’s requirements. The Embassy of Croatia may request additional documents, and this may lead to further delay in visa application processing time.
Applications will be accepted maximum 90 days prior to the travel date.
Please be sure to submit your visa application well in advance, to avoid any inconveniences.

## Download Forms
Visa Application Form duly filled Online only in English & signed by the applicant.
(For minors under age of 18: signed by parent(s) or legal guardian)
Online Application Form (https://crovisa.mvep.hr/?lang=en)
- Uploading Photograph is a must.
- Uploading the supporting documents as per the document checklist is a must.
- All items on the application are mandatory;
- Hostel/ Hotel Details and Contact Details
- Contact Details for the applicant.
- Not Uploading documents will affect your visa application decision.

visa_types.overview:
Overview
Short-term visa (visa C) - approval for transit through or intended stay in the territory of Croatia for a duration of no more than 90 days in any 180-day period.
It is issued for one, two or more entries for transit, tourist, business, private or other purposes. The validity period of a visa depends on the circumstances of traveling and cannot exceed five years.
The third-country national may not work in the territory of Croatia based on a short-term visa (visa C) alone.
Short-term visa (visa C) application shall be submitted in person. Visa application shall be submitted no more than six months.
All third-country nationals who are holders of valid Multiple Entry Schengen visa, as well as national visas and residence permits of Bulgaria, Cyprus, and Romania do not require an additional (Croatian) visa for Croatia. They can transit or stay in the territory of Croatia not exceeding 90 days in any 180-day period.
visa_types.full_text:
## Overview
Short-term visa (visa C) - approval for transit through or intended stay in the territory of Croatia for a duration of no more than 90 days in any 180-day period.
It is issued for one, two or more entries for transit, tourist, business, private or other purposes. The validity period of a visa depends on the circumstances of traveling and cannot exceed five years.
The third-country national may not work in the territory of Croatia based on a short-term visa (visa C) alone.
Short-term visa (visa C) application shall be submitted in person. Visa application shall be submitted no more than six months.
All third-country nationals who are holders of valid Multiple Entry Schengen visa, as well as national visas and residence permits of Bulgaria, Cyprus, and Romania do not require an additional (Croatian) visa for Croatia. They can transit or stay in the territory of Croatia not exceeding 90 days in any 180-day period.

## Visa Fees
Fees are due on the visa application when applying.

- Apart from the visa fee, a

- Family members of a Croatian national (spouse and children),
- Family members of citizens of EEA Member States and Switzerland,
- Children up to the age of 12,
- Holders of diplomatic and service passports travelling in their official capacity
- Pupils, students, postgraduate students and teachers accompanying them when they come to attend school, study and professional training,
- Researchers coming to conduct research, as provided for by the Recommendation of the European Parliament and of the Council of 28 September 2005 to facilitate the issue by the Member States of uniform short-stay visas for researchers from third countries travelling within the Community for the purpose of carrying out scientific research,
- Representatives of non-profit organizations up to the age of 25, who are to attend seminars, conferences, sport, cultural or educational events organized by non-profit organizations.

## Photo specifications

visa_types.name: LONG TERM (VISA D) - Study
visa_types.documents_required: Click here to download the checklist. (#)
visa_types.visa_fees:
Fees are due on the visa application when applying.

[Table:   LONG TERM (VISA D) -  Study]
Service Fee:
- Apart from the visa fee, a
VFS service charge of NIS 139.47
inclusive of VAT per application will be levied for applicants applying at the visa application centre.
- The applicable visa fee in NIS/ILS is as per the current exchange rate. It is subject to change without notice due to sudden foreign exchange fluctuations.
- All fees are non-refundable.
The following are exempt from long-term visa (visa D) fees upon application:
- children up to the age of 12,
- family reunification – exempted in the case of family members of a Croatian national (spouse, children)
- third-country nationals who have been granted the temporary stay for the following purposes:
- secondary school education
- university studies
- research
- humanitarian grounds.

visa_types.overview:
Overview
A long-stay visa (D visa) is an entry permit if the third-country national has been approved for a temporary residence permit or a residence and work permit and if a visa is required to enter the Republic of Croatia.
An application for a category “D” visa is submitted in person.
visa_types.full_text:
## Overview
A long-stay visa (D visa) is an entry permit if the third-country national has been approved for a temporary residence permit or a residence and work permit and if a visa is required to enter the Republic of Croatia.
An application for a category “D” visa is submitted in person.

## Visa Fees
Fees are due on the visa application when applying.

[Table:   LONG TERM (VISA D) -  Study]
Service Fee:
- Apart from the visa fee, a
VFS service charge of NIS 139.47
inclusive of VAT per application will be levied for applicants applying at the visa application centre.
- The applicable visa fee in NIS/ILS is as per the current exchange rate. It is subject to change without notice due to sudden foreign exchange fluctuations.
- All fees are non-refundable.
The following are exempt from long-term visa (visa D) fees upon application:
- children up to the age of 12,
- family reunification – exempted in the case of family members of a Croatian national (spouse, children)
- third-country nationals who have been granted the temporary stay for the following purposes:
- secondary school education
- university studies
- research
- humanitarian grounds.

- in colour
- 35x45 mm
